rows: The main feature of Expanded Metal is that the mesh is formed from a single piece of metal. A metal sheet, plate or foil is passed through
an expanding machine having a serrated knife which shears and stretches the metal into strands but leaves them joined at the knuckles.
This high strength-to-weight ratio material is technically functional in many protective and structural applications. The jointless expanded
metal ensures that every strand will perform as a structural member, distributing the load over a wide area.
1. No separate parts. Nothing to work losse, no fretting strands. Stays together under stress, even when cut into irregular shapes.
2. The strands are angled to the plane of the complete sheet of mesh in a truss-like formation which ensures excellent load bearing
3. Protection. The range includes meshes small enough to exclude even the smallest fingers where moving parts are within finger length
4. Screening effect - especially in anti-dazzle fences.
5. Reinforcement. The meshes anchor immovably and the angle strand formation further strengthens the reinforced material.
6. Provides anti-slip surface e.g. catwalk, stairtreads and ramps.
NOMINAL SPAN OF MESH - SIMPLY SUPPORTED
* These meshes whilst safe for the spans shown, may be springy, underfoot.
Reference to sheet size, strand width, thickness and weights are approximate only. Whilst every effort is made for these figures to be accurate, it must be
understood that the specifi cation of goods manufactured and supplied by the company is subject to normal trade tolerances.
The company policy is one of continuous development : we therefore reserve the right to alter specifications, etc without notice.
